etymology.description: 炎(炎) (yán, 'fire; flame') + phonetic 舛(舛) (chuǎn) in early forms -> detached and split, then corrupted -> 米(米) (mǐ, 'rice') in the regular script
etymology.interpretation: Initially, 粦(粦) represented 'fire' combined with a phonetic component. The character evolved over time with changes in script structure, leading to a corruption where the top eventually resembled 米(米) (mǐ, 'rice'). Despite these changes, its primary association with 'fire' remained, as indicated by its top component originally depicting flames.
